# pheno-context

> Canonical request context for the pheno-* fleet. Builder, header
> extraction, structured metadata bag.

## Public API

- `Context` — request-scoped metadata bag: `request_id`, `trace_id`,
  `tenant_id`, `user_id`, `bag` (extension field)
- `ContextBuilder` — fluent builder: `.request_id(uuid)`, `.trace_id(id)`,
  `.tenant(id)`, `.user(id)`, `.header(name, value)`, `.build()`
- `from_headers(headers)` — extract from `http::HeaderMap` (or compatible)
  using `x-request-id`, `x-trace-id`, `x-tenant-id`, `x-user-id` keys
- `current()` / `scope(ctx, future)` — task-local context propagation
  (uses `tokio::task_local!`)
- `merge(base, overlay)` — overlay wins on `request_id`/`trace_id` collision
- `ContextError` — `thiserror`-based error type

## Quickstart

```rust
use pheno_context::{Context, ContextBuilder, scope};
let ctx = ContextBuilder::new()
    .request_id(uuid::Uuid::new_v4())
    .trace_id("trace-abc-123")
    .build();
scope(ctx, async move {
    // ... downstream code can call pheno_context::current()
    Ok::<_, ()>(())
}).await?;
```

## Files

- `Cargo.toml` — package manifest (v0.1.0)
- `src/lib.rs` — Context struct, ContextBuilder, from_headers, scope
- `tests/lib_test.rs` — builder + scope tests
- `tests/async_propagation_test.rs` — task-local propagation
- `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `WORKLOG.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`
- `LICENSE-MIT` — MIT license

## Conventions

- Conventional Commits
- rustfmt + clippy (`-D warnings`)
- 80% lib coverage gate (ADR-040)
- `thiserror` for error types; no `unwrap()` in public API
- Public API surface fully documented (`#![warn(missing_docs)]`)
- `tokio` task-local for async propagation; `tracing` span linkage
  via `request_id` and `trace_id` fields
- Never blocks; never panics on missing header (returns empty string)

## See also

- `pheno-config` — typed config loader (config layer below context)
- `pheno-otel` — OTLP exporter (consumes `trace_id` for span export)
- `pheno-tracing` — tracing substrate (auto-extracts context fields)
- `pheno-port-adapter` — port adapter context injection
- `pheno-request-id` — UUID v7 generation
- ADR-015 v2.1 (worklog schema with `device:` field)
